fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Utah Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Utah - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity utah, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to utah, pharmacy technician compact utah, utah pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Utah Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing
P.O. Box 146741
Salt Lake City, Utah 84114-6741

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Utah.

steps.title: Meet Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you meet the basic eligibility criteria: be at least 16 years of age, graduate from a state-approved pharmacy technician training program OR complete 500 hours of pharmacy experience, and pass a board-approved national certification exam (e.g., PTCB's PTCE or NHA's ExCPT).

steps.title: Create an Account and Start Application
steps.description: Go to the Utah DOPL Online Licensing Portal (secure.utah.gov/account/login.html). Create a new account if you are a first-time user, or log in. Once on your dashboard, select 'New Application' and choose 'Pharmacy Technician' to initiate your application.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Upload Documents
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form. Upload all necessary supporting documentation, which typically includes official transcripts from your training program (if applicable), proof of 500 hours of experience (if applicable), and your official national certification exam results.

steps.title: Initiate and Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: As part of the online application, forms will be generated for a criminal background check. Follow the instructions provided within the DOPL portal to submit your fingerprints to the Utah Bureau of Criminal Identification (BCI) for processing. This step is mandatory for licensure.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee and Submit
steps.description: Review your entire application and all uploaded documents for accuracy and completeness. Pay the non-refundable application fee of $60.00 directly through the Utah DOPL Online Licensing Portal using a credit/debit card, and then submit your application for review.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Utah offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the Utah Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Utah by submitting a new application to the Utah Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Utah-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 1 to 3 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Utah pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Utah have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Utah. To work in Utah, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Utah?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Utah while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Utah by submitting a new application to the Utah Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Utah-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 1 to 3 Weeks.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Utah
hero.dek: Apply for a Pharmacy Technician license online through the Utah DOPL Online Licensing Portal (secure.utah.gov/account/login.html). Create an account, initiate a new application for Pharmacy Technician, and follow the specific steps provided.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Utah DOPL Online Licensing Portal. Create a new account by clicking 'Create Account' if you don't have one, or log in with your existing credentials. Once logged in, select 'New Application' from your dashboard and choose 'Pharmacy Technician' to begin the application process.
